summary: Re-ran /app/backend/tests/test_security_fixes.py after SEC-005 filename sanitization fix (re.sub(r'\.{2,}', '.', name) in _safe_filename). All 13/13 tests pass. Verified Content-Disposition filename for path-traversal payload is now 'visits_._._etc_passwd_to_x.csv' — no '..' fragments and matches strict [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ regex. CSV formula injection (=cmd|test prefixed with apostrophe) still passes. SEC-001 (JWT old-secret rejected), SEC-003 (IDOR: employee cannot view another's visits), SEC-004 (413 on >2MB payload, limit clamped to 200) all confirmed passing. Regression suite (admin login, employee checkin/checkout, admin list users/visits, CSV & PDF exports) also green.
critical_code_review_comments: _safe_filename now correctly collapses consecutive dots (line 578 /app/backend/server.py). Fix is minimal and targeted.
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures created ephemeral admin-scoped employees, setups, and visits with unique uuid-suffixed emails; no persistent seed changes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security suite is fully green (13/13). Path-traversal filename output verified as 'visits_._._etc_passwd_to_x.csv'. No outstanding SEC-00X issues.
rca_of_issue: Previous iteration: _safe_filename only replaced non-[A-Za-z0-9._-] chars, leaving '..' from '../../etc/passwd' intact after underscore substitution (e.g. 'visits_.._.._etc_passwd_to_x'). Fix adds a second pass `re.sub(r'\.{2,}', '.', name)` collapsing runs of dots to a single '.'. Verified via live curl and pytest assertion `.. not in fname`.
